results.question: What is FastAPI used for?
results.answer:
Answer:
FastAPI is used for building high-performance APIs with Python.

results.question: How do you declare path parameters in FastAPI?
results.answer:
Answer:
You can declare path parameters in FastAPI using the `Path` function from the `fastapi` module.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/markdown/fastapi-request-body.md, document_source: FastAPI, year: 2022, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000108-99914d4e6c, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fastapi/fastapi/master/docs/en/docs/tutorial/body.md

results.answer_text: You can declare path parameters in FastAPI using the `Path` function from the `fastapi` module.

results.question: What are query parameters in FastAPI?
results.answer:
Answer:
Query parameters in FastAPI are optional parameters that can be passed to a path operation function using the URL. They are declared as function parameters with default values, and their types are automatically converted by FastAPI.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/fastapi-html-dependencies.html, document_source: FastAPI, year: 2022, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000547-6ef6b0f2b0, url: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/dependencies/

3. source_file: data/raw/markdown/fastapi-query-params.md, document_source: FastAPI, year: 2022, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000098-0aa6f2ff9b, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fastapi/fastapi/master/docs/en/docs/tutorial/query-params.md
4. source_file: data/raw/markdown/fastapi-query-params.md, document_source: FastAPI, year: 2022, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000099-37bc23d0f2, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fastapi/fastapi/master/docs/en/docs/tutorial/query-params.md

results.answer_text: Query parameters in FastAPI are optional parameters that can be passed to a path operation function using the URL. They are declared as function parameters with default values, and their types are automatically converted by FastAPI.

results.question: How does FastAPI use Pydantic models for request bodies?
results.answer:
Answer:
FastAPI uses Pydantic models for request bodies by declaring function parameters to be of the type of a Pydantic model. FastAPI will then interpret these parameters as request bodies.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/markdown/fastapi-request-body.md, document_source: FastAPI, year: 2022, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000108-99914d4e6c, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fastapi/fastapi/master/docs/en/docs/tutorial/body.md

results.answer_text: FastAPI uses Pydantic models for request bodies by declaring function parameters to be of the type of a Pydantic model. FastAPI will then interpret these parameters as request bodies.

results.question: What are dependencies in FastAPI?
results.answer:
Answer:
Dependencies in FastAPI are a way for your code to declare things that it requires to work and use, which are then provided by the system (in this case, FastAPI). This is known as "Dependency Injection".

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/fastapi-html-dependencies.html, document_source: FastAPI, year: 2022, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000544-912fd063c2, url: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/dependencies/

5. source_file: data/raw/html/fastapi-home.html, document_source: FastAPI, year: 2022, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000529-2bb79b792f, url: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/
results.answer_text: Dependencies in FastAPI are a way for your code to declare things that it requires to work and use, which are then provided by the system (in this case, FastAPI). This is known as "Dependency Injection".

results.question: What is a Pydantic model?
results.answer:
Answer:
A Pydantic model is a class that inherits from `BaseModel` and defines fields as annotated attributes. It's used to define schema in Pydantic, similar to structs in other languages, and can be thought of as the requirements of a single endpoint in an API.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/pydantic-html-models.html, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000893-adf7537c60, url: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/models/
2. source_file: data/raw/html/pydantic-html-models.html, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000927-7627228c54, url: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/models/
3. source_file: data/raw/markdown/pydantic-models.md, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000269-c29df13cec, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pydantic/pydantic/main/docs/concepts/models.md
4. source_file: data/raw/html/pydantic-html-models.html, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000912-f704943c0a, url: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/models/
5. source_file: data/raw/markdown/pydantic-models.md, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000326-a32cdaa8f7, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pydantic/pydantic/main/docs/concepts/models.md
results.answer_text: A Pydantic model is a class that inherits from `BaseModel` and defines fields as annotated attributes. It's used to define schema in Pydantic, similar to structs in other languages, and can be thought of as the requirements of a single endpoint in an API.

results.question: What are Pydantic field defaults used for?
results.answer:
Answer:
Pydantic field defaults are used to specify the default value for a model field. They can also be used to validate default values.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/pydantic-html-fields.html, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000790-176414c44a, url: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/fields/

results.answer_text: Pydantic field defaults are used to specify the default value for a model field. They can also be used to validate default values.

results.question: What are Pydantic validators used for?
results.answer:
Answer:
Pydantic validators are used to validate and process data in various powerful ways. They can be customised and serialised, allowing for alteration of how data is processed.

Sources:

results.answer_text: Pydantic validators are used to validate and process data in various powerful ways. They can be customised and serialised, allowing for alteration of how data is processed.

results.question: What does Pydantic serialization do?
results.answer:
Answer:
Pydantic serialization converts a model to a dictionary or JSON-encoded string.

results.question: What is JSON Schema used for in Pydantic?
results.answer:
Answer:
JSON Schema is used for customizing and modifying the generated JSON schema in Pydantic. It allows developers to customize the JSON schema generation process, including sorting, field-level customization, and model-level customization.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/pydantic-html-json-schema.html, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000877-1271e6fdd9, url: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/json_schema/

4. source_file: data/raw/html/pydantic-html-json-schema.html, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000839-b6946463b2, url: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/json_schema/
5. source_file: data/raw/markdown/pydantic-json-schema.md, document_source: Pydantic, year: 2023, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000260-bbe10e9986, url: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pydantic/pydantic/main/docs/concepts/json_schema.md
results.answer_text: JSON Schema is used for customizing and modifying the generated JSON schema in Pydantic. It allows developers to customize the JSON schema generation process, including sorting, field-level customization, and model-level customization.

sources.section_title: Customizing the JSON Schema Generation Process

results.question: What is LangChain used for?
results.answer:
Answer:
LangChain is used for creating customizable agents that can be tailored to specific use cases and data. It provides a highly configurable harness, called `create_agent`, which allows users to compose exactly the agent they need from model, tools, prompt, and middleware.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/langchain-overview.html, document_source: LangChain, year: 2024, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000652-67956eda9e, url: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/overview

3. source_file: data/raw/html/langchain-overview.html, document_source: LangChain, year: 2024, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000651-abb2827f8d, url: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/overview

5. source_file: data/raw/html/langchain-structured-output.html, document_source: LangChain, year: 2024, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000708-346da98ff0, url: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/structured-output
results.answer_text: LangChain is used for creating customizable agents that can be tailored to specific use cases and data. It provides a highly configurable harness, called `create_agent`, which allows users to compose exactly the agent they need from model, tools, prompt, and middleware.

results.question: What are agents in LangChain?
results.answer:
Answer:
Agents in LangChain are composable, customizable, and extensible components that can be combined to create a wide range of use cases. They consist of a model, tools, prompt, and middleware, which can be composed together using the `create_agent` function.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/langchain-overview.html, document_source: LangChain, year: 2024, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000652-67956eda9e, url: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/overview

results.answer_text: Agents in LangChain are composable, customizable, and extensible components that can be combined to create a wide range of use cases. They consist of a model, tools, prompt, and middleware, which can be composed together using the `create_agent` function.

results.question: What are tools in LangChain agents?
results.answer:
Answer:
Tools in LangChain agents are Python callables, LangChain tools, or tool dicts that provide the agent with additional functionality.

results.question: What is structured output in LangChain?
results.answer:
Answer:
Structured output in LangChain is a feature that allows agents to return validated schema from the model, which can be used for various applications such as data validation and error handling.

results.question: What is short-term memory in LangChain?
results.answer:
Answer:
Short-term memory in LangChain refers to the ability of an agent to store and access information for a specific conversation. It is used to maintain thread-level persistence, allowing the agent to recall previous conversations and maintain context.

Sources:

1. source_file: data/raw/html/langchain-tools.html, document_source: LangChain, year: 2024, page: null, chunk_id: chunk-000734-b79eda40af, url: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/tools

results.question: What are Python control flow tools?
results.answer:
Answer:
Control flow tools are a set of features in Python that allow you to control the flow of your program's execution. They include if statements, for loops, while loops, break and continue statements, else clauses on loops, pass statements, match statements, and more.

Sources:

results.question: What are Python lists used for?
results.answer:
Answer:
Python lists are used for storing collections of objects of different types, similar to C or Pascal arrays. They can be thought of as a small collection of related data which may be of different types and are operated on as a group.

results.question: How are Python modules used?
results.answer:
Answer:
Python modules are used to structure Python's module namespace by using "dotted module names". They can be used to save authors from having to worry about each other's global variable names, and to provide new functions and types that can be used from Python. Extension modules can also be created in C or C++ to interface with the Python interpreter.

Extension modules can also be created in C or C++ to interface with the Python interpreter.

results.question: What are Python classes used for?
results.answer:
Answer:
Python classes are used to provide object-oriented programming features such as inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation. They can contain arbitrary amounts and kinds of data and methods that operate on that data.
